---
title: "Links usage guidelines"
---
## When to use

* Use a link to take the user somewhere: another page, another section, or an external resource. If activating it only changes location, it is a link.

* Use inline links inside prose to connect a mention to its page (for example the data process name in "created by the **Nightly import**").

* Use standalone links for contextual navigation next to content (for example **View dataset details**, **Learn more**).

* Use an external link icon when the destination leaves the product.

## When not to use

* Don't use a link to trigger an action: submitting a form, downloading a file, starting or deleting something is a [button](button). Actions change state; links change place.

* Don't dress a link as a low-emphasis action: use a ghost [button](button) instead of a link for **Cancel** or **Retry**.

* Don't use links to switch between views of the same content: use [tabs](tabs) or a [segmented control](segmented-control).

* Don't use a bare icon as a link without an accessible label.

## Variant selection

Links have no type or size variants; the choices are between modes and configurations.

* **Inline:** within a sentence or table cell; link only the specific relevant words, never the whole sentence.

* **Standalone:** on its own line, typically after or below the content it extends.

* **External:** add the external link icon; only these open outside the current context.

* **Visited:** keep the visited state visually distinct in both inline and standalone positions.

## Do / Don't

| Do | Don't |
| --- | --- |
| Reserve links for navigation, buttons for actions | Trigger a delete or a submit from a link |
| Name the destination in the link text (**View dataset details**) | Write **Click here** or paste a raw URL |
| Open in the same tab unless marked external | Surprise the user with a new tab |
| Mark external destinations with the icon | Send users out of the product unannounced |

> Link text rules (descriptive wording, consistency, sentence case, no terminal punctuation) live in the Content guidelines for links.
